stem: In triangles JKL and PQR, angles K and Q each have measure 38° and angles L and R each have measure 52°. Which additional piece of information is sufficient to determine whether triangle JKL is congruent to triangle PQR?

answerOptions.content: The lengths of sides JK and PQ
Whether triangle JKL contains a right angle

rationale: Because angles K and Q are equal and angles L and R are equal, triangles JKL and PQR are similar by AA (with J↔P, K↔Q, L↔R). To determine whether two similar triangles are also congruent, it suffices to show that one pair of corresponding sides are equal in length. JK and PQ are corresponding sides under this similarity, so knowing their lengths is sufficient.
